source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260520 우크라이나(Licarno) 23G, P1 - 이창민 팀장님/260527 1차 제출본/3.2 [Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector] Technical Document/A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports/1. [23G series] IEC 60601-1 CB Certi & Test report (ed. 3.2).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk of IEC 60601-1 report REP028549 documenting PEMS/software lifecycle, risk management, essential performance, electrical safety, component certifications, environmental/mechanical tests, leakage current, dielectric strength, and temperature results for detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory submission evidence for IEC 60601-1 basic safety and essential performance, risk management traceability, component compliance, and test results.
keyPoints: PEMS lifecycle, validation, problem resolution, IT-network instructions, and software change controls were marked pass with references to software validation and risk management files.
Risk management was traced to A-TCF-5-103, A-TCF-5-104, and FMEA table A-TCF-5-105 for ISO 14971 clauses 4.1 through 9.
Essential performance functions include intended image acquisition, avoidance of adjacent-line/blank image defects, normal operation on demand, and normal manufacturer program operation when powered.

Electrical safety testing included power input, protective earth, leakage current, dielectric strength, heat, ingress, cleaning, single-fault, and mechanical strength tests with passing outcomes where applicable.

entities.name: A1717MCW
entities.evidence: Power input, leakage current, temperature, and patient leakage tables list Model A1717MCW.

entities.name: A1417MCW
entities.evidence: Power input and temperature tables list Model A1417MCW.

entities.name: F1417MCW
entities.evidence: Power input, leakage current, and temperature tables list Model F1417MCW.

entities.name: DEPS9601 / DEPS9601A
entities.evidence: Power configuration 1 uses power supply box model DEPS9601; testing conducted with model DEPS9601A.

entities.name: BM090S15F
entities.evidence: Power configuration 2 uses AC/DC adapter model BM090S15F.

entities.name: ELT-3S1PA (3ICP5/43/97)
entities.evidence: Critical components table lists Elentec Co., Ltd. ELT-3S1PA battery pack, 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh, IEC 62133 and UL 2054.

concepts.name: PEMS development lifecycle
concepts.evidence: PEMS development lifecycle, verification milestones, validation plan, problem resolution, and IT-network connection instructions are documented and marked P.

concepts.name: Software safety classification
concepts.evidence: Software classification for software changes is stated as Software Class: B.

concepts.name: Risk management file traceability
concepts.evidence: RM results table maps ISO 14971 clauses to risk management plan A-TCF-5-103, report A-TCF-5-104, and FMEA table A-TCF-5-105 with verdict P.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Essential performance functions include intended image acquisition, no adjacent two-line or blank image, normal operation when required, and normal manufacturer program operation.

concepts.name: Leakage current compliance
concepts.evidence: Earth leakage, touch current, and patient leakage current measurements were recorded under normal and single fault conditions with Pass remarks.

concepts.name: Dielectric strength
concepts.evidence: MOOP/MOPP dielectric strength table lists insulation areas A through N with no dielectric breakdown after 1 minute.

concepts.name: Ingress and cleaning
concepts.evidence: IP54 ingress and cleaning with 70% isopropyl alcohol showed no wetting on electrical insulation and post-test dielectric/leakage compliance.

evidence.claim: The report records PEMS/software development process controls against IEC 62304:2006 and AMD1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution applied according to IEC 62304:2006 and IEC 62304:2006/AMD1:2015; references attachment Software - IEC 62304:2006 + AMD1:2015; verdict P.

evidence.claim: PEMS validation and verification planning were documented for basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 14.10 verification plan and 14.11 PEMS validation plan reference Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 8; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software changes were classified as Class B and managed under IEC 62304 clauses 5, 7, 8, and 9.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Classification for Software changes applied in accordance with IEC 62304 clauses 4.3 and 4.4: Software Class: B; process, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution for changes marked P.

evidence.claim: IT-network connection documentation included purpose, required characteristics/configuration, security specifications, information flow, and hazardous situations.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 14.13 lists IT-network purpose, characteristics, configuration, technical specifications including security specifications, intended information flow, and FMEA Hazard No. RM-24; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Usability-related risks for controls and indicators were addressed through IEC 60601-1-6 and IEC 62366-1.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.1 states equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-6 and IEC 62366-1, see attachment 3; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Secondary lithium batteries complied with IEC 62133 or IEC 62133-2, and the provided battery pack was IEC 62133 certified.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.4.3.3 states IEC 62133 certified rechargeable Li-ion battery pack is provided; 15.4.3.4 says secondary lithium batteries comply with IEC 62133 or IEC 62133-2.

evidence.claim: The report does not cover ME systems requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 16.1 states this test report does not cover requirements for ME systems and refers to general product information and other remarks; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer declared compliance with IEC 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic disturbances.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 17 states manufacturer declared equipment compliance with IEC 60601-1-2, Electromagnetic disturbances; compliance documented by manufacturer; verdict N/E.

evidence.claim: Risk management outputs were mapped to ISO 14971 clauses and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: RM results table maps ISO 14971 clauses 4.1 through 9 to A-TCF-5-103, A-TCF-5-104, and A-TCF-5-105; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Essential performance functions were defined with no unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Essential performance table lists image acquisition as intended, no adjacent two-line or blank image, normal operation when required, and manufacturer program operation; remarks: No unacceptable risks.

evidence.claim: Measured input current did not exceed 110% of ratings for listed power configurations.
evidence.evidenceText: Power input tables for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW state measured input current did not exceed 110% of unit ratings.

evidence.claim: Markings were legible and durable after water, ethanol, and isopropyl alcohol rub tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Legibility table states all markings clearly legible from operator position at 500 lx; durability table reports no curling/loosening and remained legible after distilled water, ethanol 96%, and isopropyl alcohol for 15 s.

evidence.claim: Protective earth testing was conducted with DEPS9601A and covered Canada national differences.
evidence.evidenceText: Protective earth connection table states testing conducted with power supply box DEPS9601A; 40 A / 2 min tests cover Canada national differences; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Leakage current measurements passed under normal and single fault conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Leakage current tables list earth leakage, touch current, and patient leakage measurements at 264 V 60 Hz with Pass remarks against NC and SFC limits.

evidence.claim: Dielectric strength testing showed no breakdown for listed MOOP/MOPP insulation areas.
evidence.evidenceText: Dielectric strength table lists areas A-N with 1MOOP, 2MOOP, or 2MOPP, test voltages up to 4000 Vrms, and dielectric breakdown after 1 minute: No.

evidence.claim: Critical components included certified power cords, enclosure materials, power supplies, adapter, battery, switch box, exposure switch, and PCB materials.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical components table lists standards and conformity marks for Longwell and I-SHENG cord sets, DEPS9601 power supply box, BM090S15F AC/DC adapter, ELT-3S1PA battery pack, switch box, exposure switch, and PCB.

evidence.claim: Mechanical strength tests passed for push, impact, drop, and mould stress relief.
evidence.evidenceText: Mechanical strength table reports Pass for 250 N push, 500 g steel ball impact from 1.3 m, hand-held 1 m drop, portable 5 cm drop, and 70 °C 7 h mould stress relief.

evidence.claim: Ingress of water and cleaning tests passed without wetting electrical insulation.
evidence.evidenceText: Ingress/cleaning table lists IP54 detector and cleaning by soft cloth with 70% isopropyl alcohol; no sign of wetting on electrical insulation; post-test dielectric and leakage compliance.

evidence.claim: Single fault short-circuit tests did not create hazardous situations.
evidence.evidenceText: Single fault table states EC455 short-circuit at 264 V for 10 min and rechargeable Li-ion battery output short-circuit at 11.55 Vdc for 10 min caused shutdown, no component damage, no fire/explosion, hazardous situation: No.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in this chunk.
Chunk begins mid-report and does not include certificate front matter or full product family identification.
IEC 60601-1-2 EMC compliance is manufacturer-declared and marked N/E in this chunk rather than independently evaluated here.
